intro: The clearest split is that AWS Secrets Manager has a free tier while Cycode does not. AWS Secrets Manager is Managed secrets service rotating RDS, Redshift, and custom credentials with KMS encryption and IAM; Cycode is Software supply chain security platform scanning secrets, IaC, SCA, and SAST across the entire. The data points AWS Secrets Manager at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars, and Cycode at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars. Pricing: AWS Secrets Manager is subscription from $0.02; Cycode is subscription from $1. Lock-in is low for AWS Secrets Manager and medium for Cycode, with DX at 4/5 vs 3/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 2/5. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits solo, static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: AWS Secrets Manager is for teams; Cycode is for teams; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose AWS Secrets Manager if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Cycode if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.

faq.q: Is AWS Secrets Manager cheaper than Cycode?
faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $0.02 for AWS Secrets Manager and $1 for Cycode. AWS Secrets Manager offers a free tier while Cycode does not, so initial cost favors AWS Secrets Manager. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from AWS Secrets Manager to Cycode?
faq.a: AWS Secrets Manager is tagged with low lock-in (aws-bound secrets). Cycode is tagged with medium lock-in (supply chain saas, replaceable). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, AWS Secrets Manager or Cycode?
faq.a: The dataset rates AWS Secrets Manager at 4/5 for developer experience and Cycode at 3/5, so AWS Secrets Manager scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is Cycode a good alternative to AWS Secrets Manager?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches teams, since both list the same fit. Cycode is flagged as not for solo, static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from AWS Secrets Manager to Cycode means leaving a free tier behind.
